text: Last class: the painting was sold for the highest price of the night.
Today: I have managed this holding for over ten years, and every target has been surpassed.
I have managed this holding for over ten years.
We have closed three major contracts this quarter.
We opened the Pittsburgh office last March.
The target was exceeded last year.
The groundwork has been laid for next year.
The report has been approved by the board.
So, how do you want to open with the partners? Lead with the experience, you are a seasoned manager and well-versed in the numbers.
I will. I have managed this holding for over ten years, and we have closed three major contracts this quarter alone.
Good. And the milestones? You spearheaded the turnaround, when did that finish?
I led the restructuring in 2019, and we opened the Pittsburgh office last March. Those are finished, so I report them in the past.
Exactly. And the results, keep them in the passive. You were instrumental in every deal, so what will you say?
Every target has been surpassed, and the groundwork for next year has been laid. It is the strongest year of my tenure.
Every target has been surpassed this year.

Maria Claudia, it is David in Houston, before our annual review call with the partners. I have pulled the numbers together, so here is the story of the year for you. First, the headline: the group has had its strongest year so far. We have closed three major contracts this quarter, and sales have grown by almost twenty percent since January. The turnaround we started two years ago has finally paid off. Now, the dated milestones go in the simple past: we restructured the fiscal area in 2022, we opened the Pittsburgh office last March, and we signed the new supplier agreement in June. Those are finished events at a definite time, so we report them in the past. But the results that still matter today go in the present perfect: every target has been surpassed, the groundwork has been laid for next year, and the team has delivered everything we promised. One more thing, and you know this is your habit to watch. Keep the passive for the results. Say the target was exceeded, not the target exceeded; say the report has been approved, not the report approved. On the call, lead with your experience. You have managed this holding for over a decade, you are well-versed in the fiscal side, and you have been instrumental in every deal. That experience speaks for itself. Call me before the meeting and we will run through it.

Right, let me prepare what I will say to the partners tomorrow. David is right. This has been our strongest year, and I want to tell that story clearly, in good English. So, two tenses. For my experience and the results that still count today, I use the present perfect. I have managed this holding for more than ten years. We have closed three major contracts this quarter, and sales have grown since January. The turnaround has paid off. For the finished, dated events, I switch to the simple past. We restructured the fiscal area in 2022. We opened the Pittsburgh office last March. We signed the supplier agreement in June. And the habit I always have to fix is the passive. I want to say the target was exceeded, not the target exceeded; the report has been approved, not the report approved; the groundwork has been laid, not the groundwork laid. So tomorrow: I open with my experience in the present perfect, I report the milestones in the past, I keep the passive for the results, and I let the numbers speak. I am a seasoned manager with a proven record, and I have earned this seat at the table. It is time to show it.

First, you join the company and learn it from the inside, doing the hands-on work and building your experience. Then, over the years, you lead projects and the results add up: targets are met and the groundwork is laid. Next, when a difficult moment comes, you spearhead a turnaround and the business recovers. After that, the dated milestones pile up: a deal is signed, an office is opened, a record is set. Finally, you tell the story: you describe your experience in the present perfect, you report each milestone in the past, and you keep the passive for the results that still matter today.
I have managed this holding for over ten years.
The target was exceeded last year.
The groundwork has been laid for next year.
She spearheaded the turnaround of the business.
Every target has been surpassed this year.
We have closed three major contracts this quarter.
